title: Cyclosorus excrescens
intro: Cyclosorus excrescens, also known as the excrescent cyclosorus fern, is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of various ailments, including fever, respiratory issues, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Cyclosorus excrescens include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Cyclosorus excrescens is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, often used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as to support wound healing and skin health.
taxonomy: Cyclosorus excrescens is a species of fern, and it belongs to the family Thelypteridaceae. This family is part of the larger group Pteridophyta, which includes all ferns. Within the family Thelypteridaceae, it is classified into the genus Cyclosorus. The genus Cyclosorus is further divided into several species, with Cyclosorus excrescens being one of them. The exact classification of Cyclosorus excrescens is still under review and may be subject to change as new research emerges.
